title: Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Kerala

overview.short: Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Idukki district of India's Kerala state.
overview.description: Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Idukki district of India's Kerala state. It is one of 18 wildlife sanctuaries among the protected areas of Kerala. It hosts the only rehabilitation centre for the Indian star tortoise in India. It is under the jurisdiction of and contiguous with Eravikulam National Park to the south.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thoovanam Waterfalls
topPlaces.description: Thoovanam Falls is a waterfall located near Munnar, Idukki district, Kerala, India. It is located in within the Pambar river of Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary at a distance of eight kilometers from the Marayur-Udumalai state highway.

topPlaces.name: Marayur
topPlaces.description: Marayur or Marayoor is a town in Devikulam taluk of Idukki district in the southwestern Indian state of Kerala. It is located 42 kilometers north of Munnar on SH 17 connecting Munnar with Udumalpet, Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Keezhanthoor
topPlaces.description: Keezhanthoor is a village in Idukki district in the state of Kerala, in southwestern India. The village is in the Western Ghats and is known for growing apples.

topPlaces.name: Manjampatti Valley
topPlaces.description: Manjampatti Valley is a 110.9 km2 (42.8 sq mi) protected area in the eastern end of Indira Gandhi Wildlife Sanctuary and National Park (IGWS&NP) in Tirupur District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is a pristine drainage basin of shola and montane rainforest with high biodiversity recently threatened by illegal land clearing and cultivation.

topPlaces.name: Pambar River (Kerala)
topPlaces.description: Pambar River originates near Anamudi in Idukki district of Kerala state in South India.

topPlaces.name: Chinnar River
topPlaces.description: The Chinnar River originates in the Annaimalai Hills below a grassy pass between the hills Kumarikkal Mala (8,275 ft) and Kaladekatti Malai In Idukki District, Kerala state South India. The 18 km long Chinnar river defines the East-West Tamil Nadu/Kerala state boundary along the northern edge of the Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary and the southern edge of the Indira Gandhi Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Kanthalloor
topPlaces.description: Kanthalloor is a village in Devikulam taluk, Idukki district, Kerala. It is a village nestled in the Western Ghats of India.

topPlaces.name: Kattumala
topPlaces.description: Kattumala (കാട്ടുമല) is one of the highest peaks in the Idukki district in Kerala, India. It is the second highest peak in the Eravikulam National Park after Anamudi.

items.note: Thoovanam Falls is a waterfall located near Munnar, Idukki district, Kerala, India. It is located in within the Pambar river of Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary at a distance of eight kilometers from the Marayur-Udumalai state highway.

Marayur or Marayoor is a town in Devikulam taluk of Idukki district in the southwestern Indian state of Kerala. It is located 42 kilometers north of Munnar on SH 17 connecting Munnar with Udumalpet, Tamil Nadu.
Keezhanthoor is a village in Idukki district in the state of Kerala, in southwestern India. The village is in the Western Ghats and is known for growing apples.

Manjampatti Valley is a 110.9 km2 (42.8 sq mi) protected area in the eastern end of Indira Gandhi Wildlife Sanctuary and National Park (IGWS&NP) in Tirupur District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is a pristine drainage basin of shola and montane rainforest with high biodiversity recently threatened by illegal land clearing and cultivation.
Pambar River originates near Anamudi in Idukki district of Kerala state in South India.

The Chinnar River originates in the Annaimalai Hills below a grassy pass between the hills Kumarikkal Mala (8,275 ft) and Kaladekatti Malai In Idukki District, Kerala state South India. The 18 km long Chinnar river defines the East-West Tamil Nadu/Kerala state boundary along the northern edge of the Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary and the southern edge of the Indira Gandhi Wildlife Sanctuary.
Kanthalloor is a village in Devikulam taluk, Idukki district, Kerala. It is a village nestled in the Western Ghats of India.
Kattumala (കാട്ടുമല) is one of the highest peaks in the Idukki district in Kerala, India. It is the second highest peak in the Eravikulam National Park after Anamudi.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Coimbatore International Airport (82 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (81 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thoovanam Waterfalls, Marayur, Keezhanthoor, Manjampatti Valley, Pambar River (Kerala).
